response:
number $\frac{100!}{10^{50}}$ was written as an irreducible fraction. the denominator of this fraction is the product of the prime factors of the numerator that are not canceled out by the denominator. the denominator will include the prime factors of 100! that are not present in 10^{50}. 

*** 

the prime factorization of 100! includes all the prime numbers up to 100. the prime factors of 10^{50} are 2 and 5. the denominator of the fraction will be the product of the prime factors of 100! that are not divisible by 2 or 5. 

*** 

to find the denominator, we need to calculate the exponents of the prime factors in 100! and subtract the exponents in 10^{50}. the exponents of 2 in 100! are calculated by the formula: 

$$
\left\lfloor \frac{100}{2} \right\rfloor + \left\lfloor \frac{100}{4} \right\rfloor + \left\lfloor \frac{100}{8} \right\rfloor + \left\lfloor \frac{100}{16} \right\rfloor + \left\lfloor \frac{100}{32} \right\rfloor + \left\lfloor \frac{100}{64} \right\rfloor = 50 + 25 + 12 + 6 + 3 + 1 = 97
$$

the exponents of 5 in 100! are calculated by:

$$
\left\lfloor \frac{100}{5} \right\rfloor + \left\lfloor \frac{100}{25} \right\rfloor + \left\lfloor \frac{100}{125} \right\rfloor = 20 + 4 + 0 = 24
$$

the exponents of 2 in 10^{50} are 50, and the exponents of 5 are 50. 

*** 

the denominator of the fraction will be the product of the prime factors of 100! that are not divisible by 2
